---
name: Feature request
about: Describe the scholarly task first; the command surface second.
title: "Feature request: "
labels: feature-request
---

## The question you cannot ask today

The scholarly task comes first. What do you want to find out, and why does
the library currently not let you ask it? A concrete example is worth a
page of abstraction — name the texts, the citation, the word, the passage,
and the answer you would expect back.

## What you tried

The closest existing commands (`nabu search`, `show`, `align`, `etym`,
`parallels`, …) and where they fall short.

## The command surface you would imagine (optional)

Only after the task is stated: a sketch of the flag or command you would
expect. This is genuinely secondary — a well-described task can usually be
served by extending an existing surface, and the maintainer will argue for
the smallest one that answers it.

## Constraints worth knowing (optional)

Anything that shapes the answer: license class of the material involved,
offline use, output format needs.
